channel.title: Our News Bahamas Podcast (Edited)
channel.description:
This is an unofficial version of the Our News podcast edited to be more consise. All rights are reserved to Our News Bahamas and Cable Bahamas Ltd. You can find the original podcast here on Apple Podcasts.

Our News, your daily one-hour passport to the latest updates and happenings. From breaking news to in-depth features, join us as we navigate through the headlines, providing you with a comprehensive view of the day's events. Stay informed, stay connected – Our News is your trusted source for the pulse of The Bahamas, delivered every day. Visit https://www.ournews.bs for more Bahamas news any time.
channel.copyright: Copyright ©2024 Our News Bahamas. All rights reserved.
channel.itunes:summary:
This is an unofficial version of the Our News podcast edited to be more consise. All rights are reserved to Our News Bahamas and Cable Bahamas Ltd. You can find the original podcast here on Apple Podcasts.

Our News, your daily one-hour passport to the latest updates and happenings. From breaking news to in-depth features, join us as we navigate through the headlines, providing you with a comprehensive view of the day's events. Stay informed, stay connected – Our News is your trusted source for the pulse of The Bahamas, delivered every day. Visit https://www.ournews.bs for more Bahamas news any time.
channel.itunes:author: Our News Bahamas (Unofficial)

item.title: Eight-Year-Old Boy Airlifted to Florida After Suffering Serious Head Injuries in North Bimini Shooting — Sunday, May 24th, 2026
item.itunes:title: Eight-Year-Old Boy Airlifted to Florida After Suffering Serious Head Injuries in North Bimini Shooting — Sunday, May 24th, 2026
item.description: Tonight on Our News Weekend, a community is in shock after an eight-year-old boy is airlifted for emergency treatment following a tragic shooting in North Bimini. We dive into the political fallout as a former House Speaker reveals what he believes triggered a crushing election defeat and calls for major electoral reform. Our team also follows the US Ambassador’s visit to BTVI to expand student opportunities and takes you under the waves for the unveiling of Grand Bahama’s first underwater sculpture museum. Watch the full broadcast for these stories, a record-shattering performance from Devyn Charlton, and a special look at the mental health legacy of Hurricane Dorian. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.ns2:encoded: Tonight on Our News Weekend, a community is in shock after an eight-year-old boy is airlifted for emergency treatment following a tragic shooting in North Bimini. We dive into the political fallout as a former House Speaker reveals what he believes triggered a crushing election defeat and calls for major electoral reform. Our team also follows the US Ambassador’s visit to BTVI to expand student opportunities and takes you under the waves for the unveiling of Grand Bahama’s first underwater sculpture museum. Watch the full broadcast for these stories, a record-shattering performance from Devyn Charlton, and a special look at the mental health legacy of Hurricane Dorian. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.itunes:summary: Tonight on Our News Weekend, a community is in shock after an eight-year-old boy is airlifted for emergency treatment following a tragic shooting in North Bimini. We dive into the political fallout as a former House Speaker reveals what he believes triggered a crushing election defeat and calls for major electoral reform. Our team also follows the US Ambassador’s visit to BTVI to expand student opportunities and takes you under the waves for the unveiling of Grand Bahama’s first underwater sculpture museum. Watch the full broadcast for these stories, a record-shattering performance from Devyn Charlton, and a special look at the mental health legacy of Hurricane Dorian. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. item.itunes:author: Our News Bahamas

item.title: Body of Missing 56-Year-Old Barbara Lewis Discovered in Grand Bahama Following Intensive Search — Monday, May 25th, 2026
item.itunes:title: Body of Missing 56-Year-Old Barbara Lewis Discovered in Grand Bahama Following Intensive Search — Monday, May 25th, 2026
item.description: Tonight on Our News, a community in Grand Bahama is left searching for answers after the tragic discovery of a missing woman's body, while police in Eleuthera investigate a separate death involving a man found in coastal waters. We bring you the latest developments on the eight-year-old shooting victim receiving emergency treatment in the US and the national security minister’s stern warning on firearm safety. Our team also looks into the possibility of a new "conch season" following a troubling report on overfishing and details a fresh police alert regarding fraudulent government payment scams. Stay tuned for the full broadcast to catch an inspiring story of youth outreach in our high schools and your complete national weather forecast. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.ns2:encoded: Tonight on Our News, a community in Grand Bahama is left searching for answers after the tragic discovery of a missing woman's body, while police in Eleuthera investigate a separate death involving a man found in coastal waters. We bring you the latest developments on the eight-year-old shooting victim receiving emergency treatment in the US and the national security minister’s stern warning on firearm safety. Our team also looks into the possibility of a new "conch season" following a troubling report on overfishing and details a fresh police alert regarding fraudulent government payment scams. Stay tuned for the full broadcast to catch an inspiring story of youth outreach in our high schools and your complete national weather forecast. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.itunes:summary: Tonight on Our News, a community in Grand Bahama is left searching for answers after the tragic discovery of a missing woman's body, while police in Eleuthera investigate a separate death involving a man found in coastal waters. We bring you the latest developments on the eight-year-old shooting victim receiving emergency treatment in the US and the national security minister’s stern warning on firearm safety. Our team also looks into the possibility of a new "conch season" following a troubling report on overfishing and details a fresh police alert regarding fraudulent government payment scams. item.itunes:author: Our News Bahamas

item.title: Nurse union, Ebola, no-bid contracts
item.itunes:title: 12-Year-Old Boy and 40-Year-Old Man Arraigned in Court Following Tragic Shooting of Eight-Year-Old in Bimini — Thursday, May 28th, 2026
item.description: Tonight on Our News, legal proceedings begin following a tragic shooting in Bimini as a 12-year-old boy and a 40-year-old man face a magistrate’s court in connection with the incident. We investigate the growing debate over the latest national budget, from the Nurses Union’s concerns about crumbling clinics to Family Island residents questioning the timeline for promised fire equipment. Our team also brings you an update on the Ebola monitoring situation at Princess Margaret Hospital and the Finance Minister’s defense of controversial government contract practices. Join us for the full broadcast for these headlines, highlights from the Best of the Best music festival in Miami, and your complete weekend weather outlook. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.ns2:encoded: Tonight on Our News, legal proceedings begin following a tragic shooting in Bimini as a 12-year-old boy and a 40-year-old man face a magistrate’s court in connection with the incident. We investigate the growing debate over the latest national budget, from the Nurses Union’s concerns about crumbling clinics to Family Island residents questioning the timeline for promised fire equipment. Our team also brings you an update on the Ebola monitoring situation at Princess Margaret Hospital and the Finance Minister’s defense of controversial government contract practices. Join us for the full broadcast for these headlines, highlights from the Best of the Best music festival in Miami, and your complete weekend weather outlook. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.

We investigate the growing debate over the latest national budget, from the Nurses Union’s concerns about crumbling clinics to Family Island residents questioning the timeline for promised fire equipment. item.itunes:author: Our News Bahamas

item.title: Murder Escape, Politician, Nurses Budget
item.itunes:title: FNM Leader Michael Pintard Demands Transparency Over Allegations Linking High-Ranking Politician to US Drug Trafficking Investigation — Saturday, May 30th, 2026
item.description: Tonight on Our News Weekend, political tensions boil over as the opposition demands answers regarding a high-profile drug trafficking investigation while a captured murder suspect faces sentencing after months on the run. We examine the latest concerns from the Nurses Union over the national budget and highlight new initiatives aimed at helping young Bahamians achieve the dream of home ownership. Our team also follows a major wave of corporate giving across the islands and takes you inside a star-studded performance at Atlantis by a three-time Grammy winner. Catch the full broadcast for these headlines, a complete sports wrap-up, and more of the news you need to know. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.ns2:encoded: Tonight on Our News Weekend, political tensions boil over as the opposition demands answers regarding a high-profile drug trafficking investigation while a captured murder suspect faces sentencing after months on the run. We examine the latest concerns from the Nurses Union over the national budget and highlight new initiatives aimed at helping young Bahamians achieve the dream of home ownership. Our team also follows a major wave of corporate giving across the islands and takes you inside a star-studded performance at Atlantis by a three-time Grammy winner. Catch the full broadcast for these headlines, a complete sports wrap-up, and more of the news you need to know. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.

item.title: Labor Day, Mechanic Shop, PACE
item.itunes:title: Labour Week Activities Officially Launch with Historic Change to Annual Parade Route — Sunday, May 31st, 2026
item.description: Tonight on Our News Weekend, Labour Week activities officially kick off with a historic change to the annual parade route while frustrated residents in a New Providence community plead for intervention over a neighbourhood mechanic shop. We celebrate the newest class of RBDF Rangers in Grand Bahama and take an emotional look at the PACE Foundation’s efforts to support teen mothers facing a cycle of abuse and trauma. Stay tuned for a critical update as the 2026 hurricane season begins, along with national soccer championship highlights and your complete holiday weekend weather outlook. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.ns2:encoded: Tonight on Our News Weekend, Labour Week activities officially kick off with a historic change to the annual parade route while frustrated residents in a New Providence community plead for intervention over a neighbourhood mechanic shop. We celebrate the newest class of RBDF Rangers in Grand Bahama and take an emotional look at the PACE Foundation’s efforts to support teen mothers facing a cycle of abuse and trauma. Stay tuned for a critical update as the 2026 hurricane season begins, along with national soccer championship highlights and your complete holiday weekend weather outlook. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. item.itunes:author: Our News Bahamas

item.title: Halkitis Resignation Calls, Davis Silent
item.itunes:title: PM Maintains Silence on Bahamian Detained in U.S. Drug Case and Alleged Political Ties — Monday, June 1st, 2026
item.description: Tonight on Our News, Prime Minister Philip Davis remains silent as questions intensify regarding a Bahamian man detained in the United States and his alleged connections to a local politician. We examine the growing calls for the Finance Minister’s resignation and provide essential preparedness updates as the 2026 Atlantic hurricane season officially gets underway. Our team also takes you inside the Caribbean Development Bank’s annual meeting at Baha Mar and brings you the latest on a tragic discovery in a western gated community. Stay tuned for these stories and a heartwarming community effort to support special needs education. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.ns2:encoded: Tonight on Our News, Prime Minister Philip Davis remains silent as questions intensify regarding a Bahamian man detained in the United States and his alleged connections to a local politician. We examine the growing calls for the Finance Minister’s resignation and provide essential preparedness updates as the 2026 Atlantic hurricane season officially gets underway. Our team also takes you inside the Caribbean Development Bank’s annual meeting at Baha Mar and brings you the latest on a tragic discovery in a western gated community. item.itunes:author: Our News Bahamas

item.title: Labor Harmony, Ebola Clear, Body Found
item.itunes:title: Union Leaders Divided Over Ethics of Publicly Supporting Political Parties During Labor Week — Tuesday, June 2nd, 2026
item.description: Tonight on Our News, tensions rise during Labor Week as union leaders debate the ethics of political endorsements while government officials address ongoing industrial concerns. We have the latest results from a recent Ebola monitoring situation and a full report on a tragic homicide that has rocked a quiet western neighborhood. Our team also follows the chilling new developments in the disappearance of an American woman in Abaco and a significant court victory for a man injured in police custody. Join us for these stories, plus a major cash seizure investigation and your complete tropical weather outlook. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.ns2:encoded: Tonight on Our News, tensions rise during Labor Week as union leaders debate the ethics of political endorsements while government officials address ongoing industrial concerns. We have the latest results from a recent Ebola monitoring situation and a full report on a tragic homicide that has rocked a quiet western neighborhood. Our team also follows the chilling new developments in the disappearance of an American woman in Abaco and a significant court victory for a man injured in police custody. Join us for these stories, plus a major cash seizure investigation and your complete tropical weather outlook. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. item.itunes:summary: Tonight on Our News, tensions rise during Labor Week as union leaders debate the ethics of political endorsements while government officials address ongoing industrial concerns. We have the latest results from a recent Ebola monitoring situation and a full report on a tragic homicide that has rocked a quiet western neighborhood. Join us for these stories, plus a major cash seizure investigation and your complete tropical weather outlook. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. item.itunes:author: Our News Bahamas

item.title: NIB Benefits Up, Budget Debate, CDB
item.itunes:title: NIB Beneficiaries to Receive Boost in Benefits Starting July 1st — Wednesday, June 3rd, 2026
item.description: Tonight on Our News, National Insurance beneficiaries prepare for a boost in payments as new adjustments take effect this July, while a top financial expert raises critical questions about the government's projected budget surplus. We go inside the high-stakes opening of the Caribbean Development Bank’s annual meeting at Baha Mar and bring you Finance Minister Michael Halkitis’ direct response to intensifying resignation rumors. Our team also investigates a tragic fatal crash on Fire Trail Road and clears the air on viral reports of a local principal being attacked. Stay tuned for the latest on a historic sentencing in an attempted murder case, a major hot streak for a Bahamian baseball star, and your complete tropical weather outlook as unsettled conditions persist. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.ns2:encoded: Tonight on Our News, National Insurance beneficiaries prepare for a boost in payments as new adjustments take effect this July, while a top financial expert raises critical questions about the government's projected budget surplus. We go inside the high-stakes opening of the Caribbean Development Bank’s annual meeting at Baha Mar and bring you Finance Minister Michael Halkitis’ direct response to intensifying resignation rumors. Our team also investigates a tragic fatal crash on Fire Trail Road and clears the air on viral reports of a local principal being attacked. Stay tuned for the latest on a historic sentencing in an attempted murder case, a major hot streak for a Bahamian baseball star, and your complete tropical weather outlook as unsettled conditions persist. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.itunes:summary: Tonight on Our News, National Insurance beneficiaries prepare for a boost in payments as new adjustments take effect this July, while a top financial expert raises critical questions about the government's projected budget surplus. We go inside the high-stakes opening of the Caribbean Development Bank’s annual meeting at Baha Mar and bring you Finance Minister Michael Halkitis’ direct response to intensifying resignation rumors. Our team also investigates a tragic fatal crash on Fire Trail Road and clears the air on viral reports of a local principal being attacked. Stay tuned for the latest on a historic sentencing in an attempted murder case, a major hot streak for a Bahamian baseball star, and your complete tropical weather outlook as unsettled conditions persist. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.itunes:author: Our News Bahamas

item.title: New Ministries & MP Accusations
item.itunes:title: Davis Administration Defends Largest Cabinet in History Following Creation of New Government Ministries — Thursday, June 4th, 2026
item.description: Tonight on Our News, the Davis administration faces intense scrutiny over the launch of several new government ministries and a controversial proposed amendment to the Bahamas Nationality Act. We bring you the latest on a major leadership shakeup within the Free National Movement as the party chairman confirms he’s stepping down, plus details on a high-ranking officer granted bail in a murder case. Our team also investigates the ongoing banking frustrations of local customers and a desperate plea for land to support the country's homeless populations. Stay tuned for your full Labor Day parade update and a complete holiday weekend weather outlook to keep you prepared for the days ahead. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.ns2:encoded: Tonight on Our News, the Davis administration faces intense scrutiny over the launch of several new government ministries and a controversial proposed amendment to the Bahamas Nationality Act. We bring you the latest on a major leadership shakeup within the Free National Movement as the party chairman confirms he’s stepping down, plus details on a high-ranking officer granted bail in a murder case. Our team also investigates the ongoing banking frustrations of local customers and a desperate plea for land to support the country's homeless populations. Stay tuned for your full Labor Day parade update and a complete holiday weekend weather outlook to keep you prepared for the days ahead. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.itunes:summary: Tonight on Our News, the Davis administration faces intense scrutiny over the launch of several new government ministries and a controversial proposed amendment to the Bahamas Nationality Act. We bring you the latest on a major leadership shakeup within the Free National Movement as the party chairman confirms he’s stepping down, plus details on a high-ranking officer granted bail in a murder case. Our team also investigates the ongoing banking frustrations of local customers and a desperate plea for land to support the country's homeless populations. item.itunes:author: Our News Bahamas

item.title: Labor Day and Abaco Investigation
item.itunes:title: Thousands March in Solidarity for Annual Sir Randol Fawkes Labor Day Parade — Friday, June 5th, 2026
item.description: Tonight on Our News, thousands take to the streets for a historic Labor Day celebration as union leaders reflect on the legacy of Sir Randol Fawkes and the ongoing struggle for workers' rights. We provide a major update on the international search in Abaco for a missing American woman as the investigation shifts from a missing persons case to a suspected murder. Our team also investigates a string of recent violence in the capital and examines the "brain drain" crisis discussed at the Caribbean Development Bank’s annual meeting. Plus, stay tuned for your complete holiday weekend weather forecast and a recap of the top plays from the world of sports. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.ns2:encoded: Tonight on Our News, thousands take to the streets for a historic Labor Day celebration as union leaders reflect on the legacy of Sir Randol Fawkes and the ongoing struggle for workers' rights. We provide a major update on the international search in Abaco for a missing American woman as the investigation shifts from a missing persons case to a suspected murder. Our team also investigates a string of recent violence in the capital and examines the "brain drain" crisis discussed at the Caribbean Development Bank’s annual meeting. Plus, stay tuned for your complete holiday weekend weather forecast and a recap of the top plays from the world of sports. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. item.itunes:summary: Tonight on Our News, thousands take to the streets for a historic Labor Day celebration as union leaders reflect on the legacy of Sir Randol Fawkes and the ongoing struggle for workers' rights. We provide a major update on the international search in Abaco for a missing American woman as the investigation shifts from a missing persons case to a suspected murder. Our team also investigates a string of recent violence in the capital and examines the "brain drain" crisis discussed at the Caribbean Development Bank’s annual meeting. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.itunes:author: Our News Bahamas

item.title: Shootings, Labor Day, Teacher Union
item.itunes:title: Young Father Gunned Down Near His Home in Bain Town Shooting — Saturday, June 6th, 2026
item.description: Tonight on Our News Weekend, communities in New Providence and Grand Bahama are reeling following a series of fatal shootings that have left two families in mourning. Tensions reach a boiling point in the education sector as the Bahamas Union of Teachers responds to a "shot over the bow" regarding government salary promises, while immigration officials ramp up enforcement with dozens of deportations. We also join the nation in remembering the victims of a tragic holiday accident and provide an exclusive look at the growing partnerships surrounding the multi-million dollar Royal Beach Club project. Plus, stay tuned for highlights from the baseball diamond and your complete weekend weather forecast as the first week of hurricane season continues. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.ns2:encoded: Tonight on Our News Weekend, communities in New Providence and Grand Bahama are reeling following a series of fatal shootings that have left two families in mourning. Tensions reach a boiling point in the education sector as the Bahamas Union of Teachers responds to a "shot over the bow" regarding government salary promises, while immigration officials ramp up enforcement with dozens of deportations. We also join the nation in remembering the victims of a tragic holiday accident and provide an exclusive look at the growing partnerships surrounding the multi-million dollar Royal Beach Club project. Plus, stay tuned for highlights from the baseball diamond and your complete weekend weather forecast as the first week of hurricane season continues. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.itunes:summary: Tonight on Our News Weekend, communities in New Providence and Grand Bahama are reeling following a series of fatal shootings that have left two families in mourning. Tensions reach a boiling point in the education sector as the Bahamas Union of Teachers responds to a "shot over the bow" regarding government salary promises, while immigration officials ramp up enforcement with dozens of deportations. We also join the nation in remembering the victims of a tragic holiday accident and provide an exclusive look at the growing partnerships surrounding the multi-million dollar Royal Beach Club project. Plus, stay tuned for highlights from the baseball diamond and your complete weekend weather forecast as the first week of hurricane season continues. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.itunes:author: Our News Bahamas

item.title: Triple shooting, CDB interview, labor
item.itunes:title: One Dead and Two Hospitalized in Brazen Daylight Triple Shooting on Constitution Drive — Sunday, June 7th, 2026
item.description: Tonight on Our News Weekend, a violent holiday weekend concludes with a brazen triple shooting on Constitution Drive as police investigate a series of murders and a suspicious death across three islands. We bring you an exclusive sit-down with the President of the Caribbean Development Bank on the region's battle for hurricane resilience and explore historic labor law reforms aimed at protecting the well-being of Bahamian workers. Our team also introduces you to a dedicated group of "Police Legends" caring for retired, shut-in colleagues and captures the vibrant energy of the Bahamas Masqueraders’ 11th-anniversary road march. Stay tuned for these headlines, a full weekend sports wrap-up, and your seven-day weather outlook to start your week. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.ns2:encoded: Tonight on Our News Weekend, a violent holiday weekend concludes with a brazen triple shooting on Constitution Drive as police investigate a series of murders and a suspicious death across three islands. We bring you an exclusive sit-down with the President of the Caribbean Development Bank on the region's battle for hurricane resilience and explore historic labor law reforms aimed at protecting the well-being of Bahamian workers. Our team also introduces you to a dedicated group of "Police Legends" caring for retired, shut-in colleagues and captures the vibrant energy of the Bahamas Masqueraders’ 11th-anniversary road march. Stay tuned for these headlines, a full weekend sports wrap-up, and your seven-day weather outlook to start your week. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.itunes:summary: Tonight on Our News Weekend, a violent holiday weekend concludes with a brazen triple shooting on Constitution Drive as police investigate a series of murders and a suspicious death across three islands. We bring you an exclusive sit-down with the President of the Caribbean Development Bank on the region's battle for hurricane resilience and explore historic labor law reforms aimed at protecting the well-being of Bahamian workers. Our team also introduces you to a dedicated group of "Police Legends" caring for retired, shut-in colleagues and captures the vibrant energy of the Bahamas Masqueraders’ 11th-anniversary road march. Stay tuned for these headlines, a full weekend sports wrap-up, and your seven-day weather outlook to start your week. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.itunes:author: Our News Bahamas

item.title: FM Halkidis Controversy; Violent Weekend
item.itunes:title: FM Halkidis Controversy; Violent Weekend
item.description: Tonight on Our News, the political showdown intensifies as the opposition demands the resignation of Finance Minister Michael Halkitis over controversial past associations, sparking a face-off between rival supporters in Parliament Square. We examine the critical recruitment challenges facing the Department of Corrections as officials struggle to find qualified male candidates to fill their ranks. Our team also looks into the sweeping reforms promised at the Public Parks and Public Beaches Authority following reports of massive overspending, and we bring you the latest on a string of weekend violence that has left families across the country in mourning. Stay tuned for these stories, a major milestone for the National School Breakfast Program, and your complete tropical weather outlook. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.ns2:encoded: Tonight on Our News, the political showdown intensifies as the opposition demands the resignation of Finance Minister Michael Halkitis over controversial past associations, sparking a face-off between rival supporters in Parliament Square. We examine the critical recruitment challenges facing the Department of Corrections as officials struggle to find qualified male candidates to fill their ranks. Our team also looks into the sweeping reforms promised at the Public Parks and Public Beaches Authority following reports of massive overspending, and we bring you the latest on a string of weekend violence that has left families across the country in mourning. Stay tuned for these stories, a major milestone for the National School Breakfast Program, and your complete tropical weather outlook. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.itunes:summary: Tonight on Our News, the political showdown intensifies as the opposition demands the resignation of Finance Minister Michael Halkitis over controversial past associations, sparking a face-off between rival supporters in Parliament Square. We examine the critical recruitment challenges facing the Department of Corrections as officials struggle to find qualified male candidates to fill their ranks. Our team also looks into the sweeping reforms promised at the Public Parks and Public Beaches Authority following reports of massive overspending, and we bring you the latest on a string of weekend violence that has left families across the country in mourning. Stay tuned for these stories, a major milestone for the National School Breakfast Program, and your complete tropical weather outlook. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.itunes:author: Our News Bahamas

item.title: Derelict vehicles, BDOC, tourists arrested
item.itunes:title: Derelict vehicles, BDOC, tourists arrested
item.description: Tonight on Our News, the government launches an intensified crackdown on derelict vehicles and illegal dumping, introducing new spot fines and land acquisition to clean up local communities. Controversy brews as a top law enforcement official faces backlash over remarks regarding the recruitment of male and female officers, while a man is hauled before the courts for an alleged high-priced scam using a forged cabinet minister's letter. Our team also brings you the details on a chaotic downtown brawl involving American tourists that left multiple officers injured and examines the regional ripple effects of a massive 6.1 magnitude earthquake. Join us for these stories, a look at the frustrations over ongoing power outages, and a celebration of a Bahamian basketball icon’s historic achievement. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.ns2:encoded: Tonight on Our News, the government launches an intensified crackdown on derelict vehicles and illegal dumping, introducing new spot fines and land acquisition to clean up local communities. Controversy brews as a top law enforcement official faces backlash over remarks regarding the recruitment of male and female officers, while a man is hauled before the courts for an alleged high-priced scam using a forged cabinet minister's letter. Our team also brings you the details on a chaotic downtown brawl involving American tourists that left multiple officers injured and examines the regional ripple effects of a massive 6.1 magnitude earthquake. Join us for these stories, a look at the frustrations over ongoing power outages, and a celebration of a Bahamian basketball icon’s historic achievement. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.itunes:summary: Tonight on Our News, the government launches an intensified crackdown on derelict vehicles and illegal dumping, introducing new spot fines and land acquisition to clean up local communities. Controversy brews as a top law enforcement official faces backlash over remarks regarding the recruitment of male and female officers, while a man is hauled before the courts for an alleged high-priced scam using a forged cabinet minister's letter. Our team also brings you the details on a chaotic downtown brawl involving American tourists that left multiple officers injured and examines the regional ripple effects of a massive 6.1 magnitude earthquake. Join us for these stories, a look at the frustrations over ongoing power outages, and a celebration of a Bahamian basketball icon’s historic achievement. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.itunes:author: Our News Bahamas

item.title: Apology, BPL Clash, PAC & Tourist Death
item.itunes:title: Apology, BPL Clash, PAC & Tourist Death
item.description: Tonight on Our News, the Department of Corrections Commissioner issues a formal apology following a public outcry over comments regarding female recruits, while a high-stakes showdown erupts in the House of Assembly over an attempt to table a controversial US affidavit. Tensions continue to boil in Parliament as the House Speaker stands her ground in a fiery clash with the opposition over the Public Accounts Committee’s mandate and the future of government oversight. We also bring you the latest on a tragic snorkeling accident off West Bay Street and the government's defense of the local egg industry against rising international competition. Catch the full report for these stories, the latest results from the baseball diamond, and your complete tropical weather outlook. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.ns2:encoded: Tonight on Our News, the Department of Corrections Commissioner issues a formal apology following a public outcry over comments regarding female recruits, while a high-stakes showdown erupts in the House of Assembly over an attempt to table a controversial US affidavit. Tensions continue to boil in Parliament as the House Speaker stands her ground in a fiery clash with the opposition over the Public Accounts Committee’s mandate and the future of government oversight. We also bring you the latest on a tragic snorkeling accident off West Bay Street and the government's defense of the local egg industry against rising international competition. Catch the full report for these stories, the latest results from the baseball diamond, and your complete tropical weather outlook. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.itunes:summary: Tonight on Our News, the Department of Corrections Commissioner issues a formal apology following a public outcry over comments regarding female recruits, while a high-stakes showdown erupts in the House of Assembly over an attempt to table a controversial US affidavit. Tensions continue to boil in Parliament as the House Speaker stands her ground in a fiery clash with the opposition over the Public Accounts Committee’s mandate and the future of government oversight. We also bring you the latest on a tragic snorkeling accident off West Bay Street and the government's defense of the local egg industry against rising international competition. Catch the full report for these stories, the latest results from the baseball diamond, and your complete tropical weather outlook. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.itunes:author: Our News Bahamas

item.title: House brawl, road rehab, cruise violence
item.itunes:title: House brawl, road rehab, cruise violence
item.description: Tonight on Our News, tempers flare in the House of Assembly for a second straight day as a heated debate over campaign contributions leads to a direct confrontation between the House Speaker and the Long Island MP. We break down the government's massive $98 million investment into national road rehabilitation and examine the Tourism Minister’s stern response to a violent brawl involving cruise passengers. Our team also investigates a tragic snorkeling incident off West Bay Street and follows the Coalition of Independents as they take their 2026 general election grievances to the courts. Catch the full newscast for these stories, a major airlift update, and your complete tropical weather outlook. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.ns2:encoded: Tonight on Our News, tempers flare in the House of Assembly for a second straight day as a heated debate over campaign contributions leads to a direct confrontation between the House Speaker and the Long Island MP. We break down the government's massive $98 million investment into national road rehabilitation and examine the Tourism Minister’s stern response to a violent brawl involving cruise passengers. Our team also investigates a tragic snorkeling incident off West Bay Street and follows the Coalition of Independents as they take their 2026 general election grievances to the courts. Catch the full newscast for these stories, a major airlift update, and your complete tropical weather outlook. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.itunes:summary: Tonight on Our News, tempers flare in the House of Assembly for a second straight day as a heated debate over campaign contributions leads to a direct confrontation between the House Speaker and the Long Island MP. We break down the government's massive $98 million investment into national road rehabilitation and examine the Tourism Minister’s stern response to a violent brawl involving cruise passengers. Our team also investigates a tragic snorkeling incident off West Bay Street and follows the Coalition of Independents as they take their 2026 general election grievances to the courts. Catch the full newscast for these stories, a major airlift update, and your complete tropical weather outlook. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. item.itunes:author: Our News Bahamas

item.title: Gibson, FNM, ZNS, gas prices
item.itunes:title: Gibson, FNM, ZNS, gas prices
item.description: Tonight on Our News, a Grand Bahama representative brings "shameful" hospital conditions and power concerns to the floor of the House while the opposition formally calls for a police probe into a controversial U.S. affidavit. We investigate a devastating fire that has knocked the national broadcaster off the air during hurricane season and break down a million-dollar commitment to community empowerment from a major telecommunications provider. Our team also has the latest on the sting at the gas pump and a major legislative push to transform patient rights in the healthcare sector. Join us for these headlines, a first look at a hip-hop icon's upcoming island performance, and your full tropical weather outlook for the weekend ahead. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.ns2:encoded: Tonight on Our News, a Grand Bahama representative brings "shameful" hospital conditions and power concerns to the floor of the House while the opposition formally calls for a police probe into a controversial U.S. affidavit. We investigate a devastating fire that has knocked the national broadcaster off the air during hurricane season and break down a million-dollar commitment to community empowerment from a major telecommunications provider. Our team also has the latest on the sting at the gas pump and a major legislative push to transform patient rights in the healthcare sector. Join us for these headlines, a first look at a hip-hop icon's upcoming island performance, and your full tropical weather outlook for the weekend ahead. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.

We investigate a devastating fire that has knocked the national broadcaster off the air during hurricane season and break down a million-dollar commitment to community empowerment from a major telecommunications provider. Our team also has the latest on the sting at the gas pump and a major legislative push to transform patient rights in the healthcare sector. Join us for these headlines, a first look at a hip-hop icon's upcoming island performance, and your full tropical weather outlook for the weekend ahead. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. item.itunes:author: Our News Bahamas

item.title: Power, Roads, Pre-clearance & More
item.itunes:title: Power, Roads, Pre-clearance & More
item.description: Tonight on Our News Weekend, Energy Minister JoBeth Coleby-Davis alerts residents to prepare for potential summer power interruptions as BPL undergoes significant infrastructure upgrades across New Providence. We provide the latest details on a tragic marine accident in Abaco that has claimed the life of a teenager and follow a high-profile visit from the U.S. Ambassador regarding the potential return of pre-clearance services to Grand Bahama. Our team also explores a major employment push at a local job fair and speaks with experts on recognizing the signs of "functional depression" following a recent rise in suspected suicides. Join us for these headlines, a full wrap-up from the sports world, and your complete tropical weather outlook to start your week. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.ns2:encoded: Tonight on Our News Weekend, Energy Minister JoBeth Coleby-Davis alerts residents to prepare for potential summer power interruptions as BPL undergoes significant infrastructure upgrades across New Providence. We provide the latest details on a tragic marine accident in Abaco that has claimed the life of a teenager and follow a high-profile visit from the U.S. Ambassador regarding the potential return of pre-clearance services to Grand Bahama. Our team also explores a major employment push at a local job fair and speaks with experts on recognizing the signs of "functional depression" following a recent rise in suspected suicides. Join us for these headlines, a full wrap-up from the sports world, and your complete tropical weather outlook to start your week. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.itunes:summary: Tonight on Our News Weekend, Energy Minister JoBeth Coleby-Davis alerts residents to prepare for potential summer power interruptions as BPL undergoes significant infrastructure upgrades across New Providence. We provide the latest details on a tragic marine accident in Abaco that has claimed the life of a teenager and follow a high-profile visit from the U.S. Ambassador regarding the potential return of pre-clearance services to Grand Bahama. Our team also explores a major employment push at a local job fair and speaks with experts on recognizing the signs of "functional depression" following a recent rise in suspected suicides. Join us for these headlines, a full wrap-up from the sports world, and your complete tropical weather outlook to start your week. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.itunes:author: Our News Bahamas

item.title: Junkanoo Fire, Nursing Scandal, Minister Backed
item.itunes:title: Junkanoo Fire, Nursing Scandal, Minister Backed
item.description: Tonight on Our News Weekend, investigators are on the scene at Junkanoo Beach following a late-night structural fire at the popular destination, while Progressive Liberal Party MPs rally behind Finance Minister Michael Halkitis amid growing calls for his resignation. We dive into the controversy surrounding a Grand Bahama nursing program as students and administrators speak out, and bring you the details of a major drug crackdown resulting in multiple arrests across the northern islands. Our team also examines the potential threat of international tariffs on the local fishing industry and takes you to the heart of the 37th annual Pineapple Festival in Eleuthera. Plus, stay tuned for the latest highlights from the sports world and a full tropical weather outlook as a new moisture plume approaches the islands. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.ns2:encoded: Tonight on Our News Weekend, investigators are on the scene at Junkanoo Beach following a late-night structural fire at the popular destination, while Progressive Liberal Party MPs rally behind Finance Minister Michael Halkitis amid growing calls for his resignation. We dive into the controversy surrounding a Grand Bahama nursing program as students and administrators speak out, and bring you the details of a major drug crackdown resulting in multiple arrests across the northern islands. Our team also examines the potential threat of international tariffs on the local fishing industry and takes you to the heart of the 37th annual Pineapple Festival in Eleuthera. Plus, stay tuned for the latest highlights from the sports world and a full tropical weather outlook as a new moisture plume approaches the islands. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.itunes:summary: Tonight on Our News Weekend, investigators are on the scene at Junkanoo Beach following a late-night structural fire at the popular destination, while Progressive Liberal Party MPs rally behind Finance Minister Michael Halkitis amid growing calls for his resignation. We dive into the controversy surrounding a Grand Bahama nursing program as students and administrators speak out, and bring you the details of a major drug crackdown resulting in multiple arrests across the northern islands. Our team also examines the potential threat of international tariffs on the local fishing industry and takes you to the heart of the 37th annual Pineapple Festival in Eleuthera. Plus, stay tuned for the latest highlights from the sports world and a full tropical weather outlook as a new moisture plume approaches the islands. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.itunes:author: Our News Bahamas

item.title: Drug Scandal, Budget Debate, AI, Sports
item.itunes:title: Drug Scandal, Budget Debate, AI, Sports
item.description: Tonight on Our News, the political firestorm in the House of Assembly reaches a new peak as the Speaker moves to expunge records amid explosive allegations involving a "politician number one" and a US drug investigation. We investigate a suspected arson attack that has left a local family homeless and bring you the details of a major six-figure drug bust on the high seas near Andros. Our team also examines a stark new security warning from the US Embassy regarding watercraft rentals and reports on a multi-million dollar acquisition set to revitalize a key tourism hub in Grand Bahama. Plus, stay tuned for a critical heat advisory update and the latest highlights from the world of sports. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.ns2:encoded: Tonight on Our News, the political firestorm in the House of Assembly reaches a new peak as the Speaker moves to expunge records amid explosive allegations involving a "politician number one" and a US drug investigation. We investigate a suspected arson attack that has left a local family homeless and bring you the details of a major six-figure drug bust on the high seas near Andros. Our team also examines a stark new security warning from the US Embassy regarding watercraft rentals and reports on a multi-million dollar acquisition set to revitalize a key tourism hub in Grand Bahama. Plus, stay tuned for a critical heat advisory update and the latest highlights from the world of sports. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.itunes:summary: Tonight on Our News, the political firestorm in the House of Assembly reaches a new peak as the Speaker moves to expunge records amid explosive allegations involving a "politician number one" and a US drug investigation. We investigate a suspected arson attack that has left a local family homeless and bring you the details of a major six-figure drug bust on the high seas near Andros. Our team also examines a stark new security warning from the US Embassy regarding watercraft rentals and reports on a multi-million dollar acquisition set to revitalize a key tourism hub in Grand Bahama. item.itunes:author: Our News Bahamas

item.title: Power Outages, Worker Death, ILO
item.itunes:title: Power Outages, Worker Death, ILO
item.description: Tonight on Our News, the Energy Minister breaks her silence on the recent wave of power outages as a massive $130 million substation project reaches its final stages. We investigate a tragic morning commute on John F. Kennedy Drive that left a public worker dead and bring you the government’s unreserved apology following a historic Privy Council ruling. Local jet ski operators are also firing back at a new US Embassy security warning, while a man faces the court for a string of violent crimes. Catch the full report for these stories, a critical heat advisory update, and the latest highlights from the world of sports. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.ns2:encoded: Tonight on Our News, the Energy Minister breaks her silence on the recent wave of power outages as a massive $130 million substation project reaches its final stages. We investigate a tragic morning commute on John F. Kennedy Drive that left a public worker dead and bring you the government’s unreserved apology following a historic Privy Council ruling. Local jet ski operators are also firing back at a new US Embassy security warning, while a man faces the court for a string of violent crimes. Catch the full report for these stories, a critical heat advisory update, and the latest highlights from the world of sports. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.itunes:summary: Tonight on Our News, the Energy Minister breaks her silence on the recent wave of power outages as a massive $130 million substation project reaches its final stages. We investigate a tragic morning commute on John F. Kennedy Drive that left a public worker dead and bring you the government’s unreserved apology following a historic Privy Council ruling. Local jet ski operators are also firing back at a new US Embassy security warning, while a man faces the court for a string of violent crimes. Catch the full report for these stories, a critical heat advisory update, and the latest highlights from the world of sports. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. item.itunes:author: Our News Bahamas

item.title: Gardner Denied Bail; Political Scandal
item.itunes:title: Gardner Denied Bail; Political Scandal
item.description: Tonight on Our News, a New York judge delivers a major blow to convicted drug trafficker Eric Player Gardner while explosive claims of a "politician one" drug scandal send shockwaves through the House of Assembly. We bring you the latest on two police officers facing extortion charges and the tragic loss of a well-known unionist in a heinous act of violence. Our team also investigates the unbearable power outages gripping Eleuthera and provides critical safety tips as a dangerous heat wave sends temperatures soaring across the islands. Stay tuned for these stories, a look at the future of parliamentary procedure, and your complete tropical weather and sports updates. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.ns2:encoded: Tonight on Our News, a New York judge delivers a major blow to convicted drug trafficker Eric Player Gardner while explosive claims of a "politician one" drug scandal send shockwaves through the House of Assembly. We bring you the latest on two police officers facing extortion charges and the tragic loss of a well-known unionist in a heinous act of violence. Our team also investigates the unbearable power outages gripping Eleuthera and provides critical safety tips as a dangerous heat wave sends temperatures soaring across the islands. Stay tuned for these stories, a look at the future of parliamentary procedure, and your complete tropical weather and sports updates. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.itunes:summary: Tonight on Our News, a New York judge delivers a major blow to convicted drug trafficker Eric Player Gardner while explosive claims of a "politician one" drug scandal send shockwaves through the House of Assembly. We bring you the latest on two police officers facing extortion charges and the tragic loss of a well-known unionist in a heinous act of violence. Our team also investigates the unbearable power outages gripping Eleuthera and provides critical safety tips as a dangerous heat wave sends temperatures soaring across the islands. Stay tuned for these stories, a look at the future of parliamentary procedure, and your complete tropical weather and sports updates. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.itunes:author: Our News Bahamas

item.title: Gardner, travel, Gibson trial
item.itunes:title: Gardner, travel, Gibson trial
item.description: Tonight on Our News, the political showdown intensifies as the opposition demands clarity on who is funding the legal defense for a Bahamian detained in the United States on drug charges. We also bring you the latest on three law enforcement officers hauled before the courts in a major cocaine trafficking conspiracy and provide an update on the expungement of criminal records for hundreds of Bahamians. Our team also investigates the latest parliamentary clash over government travel expenditures and highlights a heartwarming $10,000 donation to support special needs students in Grand Bahama. Join us for these headlines, the latest from the track and field nationals, and your complete tropical weather outlook as the record-breaking heat wave continues. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.ns2:encoded: Tonight on Our News, the political showdown intensifies as the opposition demands clarity on who is funding the legal defense for a Bahamian detained in the United States on drug charges. We also bring you the latest on three law enforcement officers hauled before the courts in a major cocaine trafficking conspiracy and provide an update on the expungement of criminal records for hundreds of Bahamians. Our team also investigates the latest parliamentary clash over government travel expenditures and highlights a heartwarming $10,000 donation to support special needs students in Grand Bahama. Join us for these headlines, the latest from the track and field nationals, and your complete tropical weather outlook as the record-breaking heat wave continues. ----- Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
